hypothesis: In SMILES–electron-density contrastive pretraining, incorporating mirror-reflected electron-density grids of chiral molecules as explicit hard negatives will produce embeddings that are SO(3)-invariant but reflection-sensitive, increasing Top-1 accuracy on an enantiomer-disambiguation retrieval benchmark by at least 15 percentage points relative to the current model while reducing functional-group Recall@10 by no more than 1 percentage point.
